g.text: “Not for strict asceticism, but for virtue, knowledge, wisdom, the truths, and the path — my disciples revere me.”
aññe ca pañca dhammā yehi mamaṁ sāvakā … upanissāya viharanti
In the debating hall: whose disciples truly honor their teacher and dwell relying on him?
Honored, revered, relied upon — at his teaching not even a cough is heard; the great crowd waits expectantly,
“as at a crossroads for pure honey free of wax.”
khuddamadhuṁ anelakaṁ · neva … khipitasaddo vā ukkāsitasaddo vā
“How many qualities do you see in me, Udāyi, for which my disciples revere me?”
Kati pana tvaṁ, udāyi, mayi dhamme samanupassasi …
THE REFRAME — WHY THE BUDDHA IS REVERED
What people assume (✗) versus what is actually so (✓)
rejected — his disciples are stricter than he is
why they truly honor and rely on him
…yet some disciples eat a cupful or less;
I sometimes eat the full contents of this bowl.
…yet some wear rag-robes from charnel grounds;
I sometimes wear soft householder robes.
I sometimes eat fine invited meals.
…yet some live at tree roots, in the open air;
I sometimes dwell in plastered stilt-houses.
…yet some plunge into deep forest tracts;
I sometimes dwell crowded by the assembly and kings.
“Endowed with the supreme aggregate of virtue.”
“I know only when I know; I teach with direct knowledge,
None can foresee a debate to refute him; disciples never
interrupt — they look to him for instruction.
Suffering · its arising · its ending · the path —
“and he satisfies their minds with his answer.”
Practicing it, many disciples attain the perfection
“My disciples do not revere me for these five.”
THE FIFTH REASON — THE LIBERATING PATH
A refrain repeats through every set below: “thereby many of my disciples dwell having attained mastery of direct knowledge” · abhiññāvosānapāramippattā
37 QUALITIES — WINGS TO AWAKENING · BODHIPAKKHIYĀ DHAMMĀ
Four Establishments of Mindfulness cattāro satipaṭṭhānā
body · felt experience · mind · mental qualities — ardent, clearly aware, mindful
prevent · abandon unwholesome · arouse · maintain wholesome — with roused energy
Four Bases of Psychic Power cattāro iddhipādā
collectedness from aspiration · energy · mind · investigation, with intentional effort
faith · energy · mindfulness · collectedness · wisdom — leading to tranquility, to awakening
the same five as unshakeable strengths — faith · energy · mindfulness · collectedness · wisdom
mindfulness · investigation · energy · joy · tranquility · collectedness · equanimity — vivekanissita … vossaggapariṇāmiṁ
Noble Eightfold Path ariya aṭṭhaṅgika magga
right view · intention · speech · action · livelihood · effort · mindfulness · collectedness
Possessed of form, one sees forms
Not perceiving form internally, one sees forms externally
One is resolved only upon the beautiful
Base of Neither Perception Nor Non-Perception
Cessation of Perception and What Is Felt
EIGHT BASES OF TRANSCENDENCE · AṬṬHA ABHIBHĀYATANĀNI
Each: “Having transcended them, one perceives: ‘I know, I see.’” · abhibhuyya jānāmi passāmi
Perceiving form within → external forms, limited
Not perceiving form within → external, limited
Not perceiving form within → external, measureless
Yellow — like a kanak champa flower
Perceived “above, below, across, undivided, and measureless” · uddhaṁ adho tiriyaṁ advayaṁ appamāṇaṁ
Born of seclusion (vivekajaṁ) · Imbued with uplifting joy and pleasure (pītisukhaṁ)
Internal tranquility (ajjhattaṁ sampasādanaṁ) · Unification of mind (ekodibhāvaṁ)
Free from reflection and examination (avitakkaṁ · avicāraṁ)
Born of collectedness (samādhijaṁ) · Imbued with uplifting joy and pleasure (pītisukhaṁ)
With the fading of desire for uplifting joy (pītiyā ca virāgā)
Experiencing pleasure with the body (sukhaṁ ca kāyena paṭisaṁvedeti)
Abandoning bodily pleasure and pain (sukhassa ca pahānā dukkhassa ca pahānā)
Settling of mental pleasure and displeasure (somanassadomanassānaṁ atthaṅgamā)
Eight attainments, each carrying its own simile from the discourse
This body of form, born of parents, impermanent —
and the consciousness bound up with it.
with a colored thread strung through it
From this body they create another, mind-made,
complete with all its limbs, lacking no faculty.
Simile: a reed drawn from its sheath ·
a sword from scabbard · a snake from slough
One becomes many; passes through walls; dives
through earth; walks on water; flies cross-legged.
Purified, surpassing the human range: hears both
kinds of sounds — divine and human, far and near.
Discerns a mind with lust, hatred, or delusion —
and a mind freed from them — as it is.
Simile: a young person seeing their face
in a clear mirror or bowl of water
Recalls manifold past lives, with their modes
from village to village and back
Sees beings passing away and reappearing,
Simile: a man between two houses,
Realizes the taintless liberation of mind and
liberation by wisdom and dwells in it.
Simile: a clear mountain lake — shells,
Taintless liberation of mind and liberation by wisdom
anāsavaṁ cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ … sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā upasampajja viharanti
